reviews.title: Works with iPhone 13
reviews.text:
This appears to use analog out over Lighting rather than having a DAC in the cable. Evidence is that there is volume control similar to using headphones rather than the no volume when plugged into an audio interface. Also if you touch the connector while plugged into speakers you get the sort of popping and cracking found when messing with a headphone cable.

So in summary, it just works.

reviews.title: Great for outside work
reviews.text:
Was wearing these when taking down Christmas decorations and listening to an audio book.

My neighbor thought it was ignoring him but I just didn’t hear him - these have good noise cancellation. Sound quality was great too.

reviews.title: Solid Value
reviews.text: Regular ear buds don't fit in my ears so I need the "wings." I am very happy with how they perform but they are not as "full range" as more expensive brands.

reviews.title: Good customer service
reviews.text: I had some issues with my first set and the customer service was awesome. I appreciate a company that will backup their products.

reviews.title: Love these
reviews.text: These are comfortable and easy to use. Good sound as well.

reviews.title: Good sound quality
reviews.text:
These are good quality but my left ear is uncomfortable. My ear must be a different shape. I was hoping the battery life would be better, but its ok. I use it to listen to books while i work. Take calls when needed.
Most bluetooth headsets have a delay or echo. These do not. It makes phone calls very clear.
These are the best sound quality of all but my Dewalt headsets.
The battery lasts about 5 to 6 hours with my streaming usage. Probably last much longer with just calls.
My left ear hurts after a couple hours, but ny right ear is fine for all day.
They charge quickly about an hour maybe a little more.
DON'T MISPLACE THE CHARGING CASE they will become worthless. They can't be charged without it.
And it seems if they power off by themselves they have to be reset in the case.
All that being said i probably would buy them again.

reviews.title: saved my husband’s life!
reviews.text: ...because it covered the sound of his snoring and I no longer am tempted to smother him with a pillow! They are very comfortable even though I’m very sensitive to irritable feelings. I’m a light sleeper and it blocked out all the little lights that keep me awake. It stayed charged all night. I’m ordering another for day time use! I’ll probably give  them to every one I know for Christmas. Seriously amazing!

reviews.title: Adequate sound quality but difficult controls.
reviews.text: I use the sleep headphones because I listen to books to get to sleep. I need the volume quite high because I use hearing aids. This headset is adequate for my purpose. It’s very comfortable to wear, but the controls are not very sensitive.

reviews.title: Works well for sleeping
reviews.text:
I wanted some headphones that were flat and wouldn't hurt my ears or fall out and get lost while I was trying to go to sleep. These work just fine. The speakers lie flatly against my head and don't poke or feel lumpy. I only use them for sleeping, though, not for exercising, so I can't say how easy or difficult it would be to remove the speakers and controller to wash the headband part.

Sound is fine. They don't go into your ear like regular earbuds, so you have to turn the volume up a little, but it does what it should. I haven't tried to control the volume using the buttons on the headband so I can't really rate that aspect. All in all, it does what I wanted it to.

reviews.title: Not comfortable
reviews.text: This thing works well, but it’s not comfortable for laying down. I was hoping to wear it at night while falling asleep but there is no way to get comfortable with it on due to the small speakers inside. I’m not sure how it would work with exercise either because it’s pretty loose and I’d be worried to get sweat on it.

reviews.title: Headband, controls, sound quality, charge stats--all exactly what I wanted
reviews.text:
This resolved so many issues with other options. The headband is so very comfortable--wish I could buy 5 headbands without speakers just for the comfort for everyday wear. What I really wanted was comfortable speakers for podcasts as I go to sleep or do tasks or just enjoy! These fit the bill. Volume is perfect. Bluetooth connection is quick and stable. I can do 2 nights before charging again. The buttons work well with podcasts, moving to next or previous episodes. I just ordered a second one.

Thank you and if there is a way to get just the headband, please let me know.

features: 【𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐨𝐥𝐮𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝟏𝟎𝐦𝐦 𝐃𝐲𝐧𝐚𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐫 𝐈𝐄𝐌】The 7Hz Salnotes Zero is a revolutionary earphone to be fitted with a 10mm dynamic driver with metal composite diaphragm. The metallic composite diaphragm used by 7Hz Zero is made of high quality materials, making it easier to resonate or vibrate along with sound waves. It was constructed with ease of use in mind as each part has been designed with precision accuracy so that they will not hinder audio transmission.

【𝐄𝐫𝐠𝐨𝐧𝐨𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐒𝐡𝐚𝐩𝐞&𝐌𝐞𝐭𝐚𝐥 𝐇𝐨𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐠】7Hz Salnotes Zero is housed in an environment-friendly plastic chassis with a stainless steel faceplate. High precision and precision measurements based on ergonomics were taken to help create an earphone that offers excellent acoustic performance, as well as safety, comfort, and durability.

【𝐃𝐞𝐭𝐚𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞 𝐇𝐢𝐠𝐡 𝐏𝐮𝐫𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐎𝐅𝐂 𝐂𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞】The earphones include a detachable cable with gold plated 0.78mm 2Pin connectors. The cable is made of 4 core high purity Oxygen Free Copper in a parallel structure while each core is made of 19*0.08 wires+250D to maximally avoid signal loss and ensure a high fidelity transmission

【𝐍𝟓𝟐 𝐌𝐚𝐠𝐧𝐞𝐭, 𝐅𝐢𝐧𝐞 𝐓𝐮𝐧𝐢𝐧𝐠】7Hz Salnote ZERO makes the ultimate audio experience available to you from the convenience of your mobile device, laptop, or desktop computer. The fine-tuning technique and use of a dynamic driver ensure accurate and efficient transmission of audio signals. You can enjoy a smooth and accurate sound without any distortion.

【𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝐃𝐞𝐩𝐭𝐡 𝐨𝐟 𝐙𝐄𝐑𝐎】By naming it ZERO, we intended to deliver our philosophy of life to every customer. Previously our team has brought Timeless and Eternal to the community, now it’s time for ZERO. ZERO means eternity and mortality without ultimateness. ZERO provides limitless imagination and allows boundless possibilities. Also, ZERO is manufactured in more than one color. Three technicolored colors are adopted for this model to bring you a vibrant atmosphere and rejuvenate your music experience.

reviews.title: The Moondrop Chu is dead. Long live the new king.
reviews.text:
Well the Moondrop Chu's reign didn't last long. These WILDLY outclass everything under $50. They wildly outclass a lot of stuff under $200.

For starters, detachable cable. I hate the fixed cable on the moondrop chu's with a passion - it's all silicone rubbery and tangles, doesn't stay in place, doesn't stay tied up in your pocket or backpack. The chu's come with a microphone, which is the only thing it has going for it, but if you REALLY need a cable, these are still a far better deal with another $10 spent on the cable. This cable is nice, comfortable, wraps up easily around 3-4 fingers and stays coiled properly.

Then, eartips. The Chu's "springtips" don't really fit quite a few people, myself included. They were so bad that the chu's sounded downright broken until I got some better tips on them. The Zero's come with about five different size of tips that were no problem to find a comfortable seal/fit.

Comfort and build wise, they're awesome. My ears can be quite picky, and they basically disappear in your head. They're light and smooth plastic, with a nice stainless steel plate on the outside. No complaints, they just work perfectly as IEM's and feel plenty durable to just live in your backpack.

Sound wise, these things really up the game. The Chu's have excellent technical performance (detail is great) but they're downright claustrophobic. It feels like every instrument in the band is licking the inside of your ear. And it's unpleasant. They're tuned quite neutral, but with no sense of space, instrument separation or imaging (that sense of more than left/right, but front/back and in between) they're just not a pleasant sound. The Zero's have IMPRESSIVE separation and imaging. Not impressive at $20. Impressive for IEM's, period. There's $300-500 stuff that doesn't do imaging and soundstage this well.

The bass could be cleaner and it doesn't quite hit the detail of the chu's (which are also punching well into the $500 range in that one specific area) but I'm nitpicking HARD. For $20, these have no right sounding so spacious, detailed (you can enjoy the detail a lot more when the instruments don't feel all laid on top of each other) and pleasant. They're not overly bassy, but they are thumpier than the chu's. No overly sparkly either. Just a very "grown up" tuning meant to make music sound nice, paired with truly outstanding soundstage.

If you play video games, there's nothing anywhere near these in the price range. You'll hear sounds ahead, behind, and how far away they are.

Songs with real detail and separation will dance around you (Bobby McFerrin, Polyphia) and show you things you didn't even know were in your music.

The only next step up I can think of from these is the $50 Truthear zero's (same model name, different brand) but they're only marginally better (with a slightly worse fit) and the $100 7hz diokos are a very noticeable step up in every way. But jeez. These things are $20. I don't understand how they're so good for that little money.

Anyone else entering the $20-$50 space better bring their A+ game. These are a tough act to follow. If you really need a microphone, these are still unbeatable with another $10-15 for a cable with one.

reviews.title: Fantastic First IEM w/ Insane Value
reviews.text:
These were my first entry into IEMs and I really enjoyed the experience. Coming from Google Pixel Buds Pros, these were a significant improvement in sound quality but given the abundance of excellent budget headphones in the chi-fi market, I can't comment as to whether they're better than any of the others you'd find for a similar price.

I also really like the USB-C 2-pin cable that it comes with. It has a subtler finish compared to twisted cables and it also has a built-in mic that works well enough for calls. I actually bought a higher end set of IEMS and ended up using the Salnotes' cable since similar cables cost 1-2x the price of the Salnotes themselves.

reviews.title: TYPE C EARBUDS!!!!
reviews.text:
They're the best quality out of all the type c buds I found on here.

Been looking for a while and found these. Before, I had a type c to 3.5 cable which kept getting in the way and having audio issues. Then got a type c adapter to 3.5 and the same thing happened. With this, its easy. Cable still gets in the way, but no audio issues.

Theyre really good earbuds. Not some 100 plus IEMS, but still great for price and sound quality. Overall these are a steal and really helpful if you haven't fallen into the wireless scam where they forced you to buy wireless earbuds by removing the jack.

Prefer cable over wireless. No brain fry.

So like...yeah it works well, good range but the touch activated button was a big no from me.

reviews.title: Difficult to connect. Oddly LOW VOLUME and INTERFERES with apps like VIBER and DUO. Easy to loose.
reviews.text:
Where do I start:::
Connecting, if you want to use it with multiple devices, you can just forget it (if connected to one device, they will not allow you to connect to another device until you actually delete the connection from the current device even if it's off, did not have this issue with Apple Pods).
SOUND LEVEL is oddly LOW. Seriously, even at max setting does not even come close to even cheap plug-in buds, so can't use them in any loud place. Noise canceling does not help with that.
If used with call apps like Viber or Duo you'll be missing every other word. Don't know how or why, but connection signal goes to crap while these are on. As soon as I switch to plug-in buds, everything works just fine.
TOO DAMN SMALL. Sure it's convenient for the ear, but trying to handle them like putting in or taking out, better be sure to do it somewhere safe in case you drop them (not outside for sure). It would be better if they had over the year clips or something.
COLLECTS all your EAR-WAX. Yes, these things go in so deep that it just gets absolutely gross (never had this problem with other buds and I clean my ears every day)

The sound is o.k., nothing to write home about, but for the price (as of this review these head phones cost $19.44) I think they are worth it.  Great for kids, not so good for adults, even if you are a pinhead.

description:

NO EAR FATIGUE.
These foam earphone tips are softer than silicone tip material, Great elasticity and recovery make the tips perfectly fit your ear canal, very comfortable.
BETTER COMFORT
Slow-rebound material, uniquely breath-activated earmuffs activated by body temperature, gives you a new experience without irritation or fatigue.
PREMIUM SOUND.
Soft foam earbud tips provide a perfect seal in the ear canal and pure audio with enhanced bass while eliminate the outside noise.
MORE ISOLATION
With the ear canal properly sealed by the earbud tips, you don't need to increase volume to enjoy music in a noisy environment.
LITTLE USING TIPS
Before wearing earbuds, squeeze the sponge tips with your hands, so they will automatically inflate and perfectly fit your ear canal after wearing. That will create better sound insulation. The memory foam is about 11mm high, the largest diameter is about 12mm, and the inner diameter of the catheter is about 3mm.Made of high-quality, environmentally friendly and non-toxic materials, it has the advantages of softer, slower rebound and more durable. Memory foam is characterized by a slow rebound effect, which can change its own shape with external pressure. From the perspective of earplugs, the ear canal varies from person to person, and the memory foam will change their shape according to the individual's ear canal, making them fit the ear canal best, thus achieving more than ordinary eartips cover good wearing comfort, better noise reduction effect, bring better listening experience. Applicable to the following earplugs: SE series:  SE110/SE112/SE115/SE210/SE215/SE310/SE315/SE420/SE425/SE530/SE535/SE846 E3C/E4C/E5C/E3G/E4G/E500/i3C/i4C/ SC series: SCL3/SCL4/SCL5/
features: 6 Pairs of replacement tips fit on 3mm cavity tube diameter for balanced armatured in-ear monitor.Please measure the outer diameter of your earplug cavity tube before purchasing, and refer to the picture to obtain the appropriate model.
The only earbud tips that adopt separate packaging; each pair is individually packed in its own little baggie; convenient for storage.
Not like other cheap earphone tips, these are high quality memory foam ear bud tips which would not fall apart or disintegrate.
Soft foam earbud tips provide a perfect seal in the ear canal and pure audio with enhanced bass while eliminate the outside noise.
Before wearing, you may need to squeeze the ear tips and put them in your ear and they expand to fit. These are pretty stretchy and stay in place.

reviews.title: Not a quality product
reviews.text: I needed these to fit my Sony earbuds which are always smaller than normal earbuds. I've used Comply in the past and loved them but they're expensive. Should have bought them anyway. These are small plastic tubes inserted into blue memory foam. The tubes don't stretch to wrap around the tip of the earbud the way quality products do and the tubes are apparently not glued to the memory foam as they separate when you're trying to install them.

reviews.title: Hold really well
reviews.text: Really like these foam tips. They hold extremely well in my ears whether I'm hitting hitting the punching bag, jogging or cutting grass.

reviews.title: The quanity you get for the price + the quality is excellent
reviews.text: I like the comfort, quality, and price of these replacement earbuds.  The foam holds up well to use, they never fall off, and they do block out sound around you but not so much you can't hear that someone spoke to you.  The price and quantity is the best on Amazon

reviews.title: Not so great
reviews.text: I bought these as an inexpensive replacement for my ear buds. They took quite a bit of twisting and pushing to get into my ear without feeling like they would fall out. With that, after just a couple days, the outer section broke away from the inner so they no longer fit on the ear bud. I do not recommend purchasing.

reviews.title: Perfect replacement size
reviews.text: Very comfortable, noise isolating, fits my older Phillips SHR 9850 earbuds like new. Like the color because you can tell when they are dirty & need to be replaced.

title: Dekoni Audio Mercury Bulletz Moldable Memory Foam Isolation Earphone Tips, Black, 4.9mm (Single Pair - Large)
features: Enjoy your earbuds even more with comfortable, noise isolating eartips from Dekoni Audio.
Always the best fit with memory foam technology that conforms to the shape of your ear for a comfortable, solid seal
Cleans easily with only soap and warm water
Available in three different sizes including 9mm, 11mm, and 13mm as well as a sample pack that includes one pair of each size

reviews.title: Comfort and Ease of Use
reviews.text: These may be the perfect tips for IEMs. I like these way more than the silicone tips. They are easy to put in and don't bother my ears. I haven't had them long so I can't speak to their durability yet. I'll come back to this review later if I have any issues.

reviews.title: Big improvement over OEM.
reviews.text: I purchased these memory foam tips to use in place of the OEM tips on my KS Z10 Pro IEMs. The seal is drastically better as these cut out almost all external sound and let you focus purely on your music. Excellent purchase.

reviews.title: I still can't get them out if my ear
reviews.text: Stuck..... stuck......STUCK

reviews.title: Great
reviews.text: Sound take a hit as earbuds itself is very thick and gives the souns a warm signature

reviews.title: Perfect fit
reviews.text: Dekoni makes a great product.
